report_name: Singapore's CSA Issues Advisory on Securing Software Supply Chains

threat_actors.name: Software Supply Chain Attackers
threat_actors.description: A general class of threat actors described in the CSA advisory who target third-party software dependencies, open-source libraries, and automated development pipelines to compromise downstream targets by hijacking accounts or publishing malicious package versions.
threat_actors.goals: Inject malicious code into software products to achieve widespread compromise of downstream targets, leading to data theft and operational disruption.

attack_patterns.name: Compromise Software Dependencies and Development Tools
attack_patterns.description: Threat actors compromise public repositories like npm or PyPI by hijacking maintainer accounts or poisoning transitive dependencies to distribute malicious code, as seen in the Axios and @ctrl/tinycolor incidents.

attack_patterns.name: Valid Accounts
attack_patterns.description: Threat actors hijack the accounts of package maintainers on platforms like npm to publish malicious versions of popular software packages.

attack_patterns.name: Compromise Host Software Binary
attack_patterns.description: As a result of a supply chain attack, malicious code is injected into a software product's binary before distribution, leading to compromise on downstream customer hosts when the software is installed or run.

tools.name: Axios
tools.description: A popular npm package that was hijacked by threat actors to distribute malicious versions, cited in the CSA advisory as an example of a software supply chain attack.

tools.name: @ctrl/tinycolor
tools.description: An npm package whose compromise led to a cascading effect on over 180 other dependent packages, cited in the CSA advisory as an example of a transitive dependency attack.

identities.name: Technology and Software Companies
identities.description: A high-risk sector identified in the CSA advisory, as they develop and distribute software that can be targeted in supply chain attacks.

identities.name: Financial Services
identities.description: A high-risk sector identified in the CSA advisory, as they build custom applications with sensitive data that rely on complex software supply chains.

identities.name: Critical Infrastructure
identities.description: A high-risk sector identified in the CSA advisory, as their operational technology may depend on software with vulnerable supply chains.

infrastructure.name: Public Code Repositories
infrastructure.description: Public software repositories like npm and PyPI are targeted by attackers to host and distribute malicious versions of legitimate packages.

indicators.name: NPM Audit Command Execution
indicators.pattern:
title: NPM Audit Command Execution
id: 2b1a987c-c45d-4f8e-8b1a-9c7d6e5f4d3c
status: experimental
description: Detects the execution of 'npm audit', a tool used to scan for vulnerabilities in software dependencies, as recommended by the CSA advisory for securing software supply chains. This can be used for compliance and security posture monitoring.
references:
  - https://www.csa.gov.sg/alerts-advisories/advisories/advisory-on-securing-the-software-supply-chain-and-development-workflows
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-04-08
tags:
  - attack.t1195.001
level: informational
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\npm.cmd'
      - '\npm'
    CommandLine|contains: 'audit'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the execution of 'npm audit', a tool used to scan for vulnerabilities in software dependencies, as recommended by the CSA advisory for securing software supply chains.

indicators.name: NPM Dependency Lock File
indicators.x_netsecio_sigma_rule:
title: NPM Dependency Lock File Presence
id: 5d8e9f0a-1b2c-4d3e-8f9a-0b1c2d3e4f5a
status: experimental
description: Detects the presence of 'package-lock.json', a file that inventories software dependencies. Its presence is normal in npm projects but monitoring its changes can be part of a supply chain security strategy.
references:
  - https://www.csa.gov.sg/alerts-advisories/advisories/advisory-on-securing-the-software-supply-chain-and-development-workflows
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-04-08
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate file in any Node.js/npm project.
level: informational
logsource:
  category: file_event
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    TargetFilename|endswith: '\package-lock.json'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the presence of 'package-lock.json', a file that inventories software dependencies and is a key artifact for conducting a supply chain security audit as per the CSA advisory.

relationships.target_name: Compromise Software Dependencies and Development Tools
